free_text: Total reaction and fusion cross sections at sub- and
near-barrier energies for the system 7Li + 28Si
the TN11/25 HVEC 5.5 MV Tandem
accelerator of the National Research Center of Greece
A 400 mu-g/cm2 thick, self- supporting natural
silicon target, tilted by 45 degrees.
Two dE-E telescopes set 12.7 cm from the
target. The thickness of the dE silicon detector
was 10 mu-m, while the thickness of the E detector
was 2000 mu-m.
Two type of measurements were performed, angular
distributions in a particle-gamma coincidence mode to
determine transfer cross sections, and single
measurements to deduce total reaction cross sections.
Ground state population was not detected.
According to author's calcualtions this is very
small and can be omitted.
Data were received from Prof.A.Pakou
Entry approved by Prof.A.Pakou
SD
Angular distribution for the pure compound exit
channel, 33S + p + n, are given in comparison
with CASCADE calculations. The difference between them
is negligible.
The compound contribution was subtracted from the
original data ('new datum' at each angle = original
datum at each angle minus the calculated compound
contribution for this angle). Calculation was done
using CASCADE code.

fusion cross sections (author's comment)
statistical uncertainty
Total reaction cross sections are obtained by summing
up cross sections of all the observed exit channels
(measurements in singles) as well as missing compound
channels. Missing compound channels, either due to low
production or due to radioactive very long-lived
residual nuclei, are determined according to CASCADE
calculations. Such channels lead to 33,31P and 27Al,
and their contribution lies in the range of 10% to 25%
of the measured cross section from higher to lower
energies. Measured channels lead to:
